req.title: Create observation (audit)
req.statement: A clinician shall create an Observation tied to a patient with LOINC-coded code element; Observations without code shall be rejected. An AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation and tested end-to-end.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-080 mandates three testable concerns: (1) a valid LOINC-coded Observation is accepted and persisted, (2) an Observation missing the code element is rejected with HTTP 422, and (3) an AuditEvent is emitted for every operation. I selected EP for the happy-path (valid partition), NEG for the missing-code rejection, BVA for the edge case of a code element present but with an empty coding array (minimum non-null boundary), DT for the audit-event emission rule across both success and failure outcomes, and STC to verify the resource lifecycle state transitions. Tests are self-contained via session-scoped fixtures for auth and patient setup, and each cleans up created resources in a finally block to remain idempotent.

tests.name: test_create_observation_with_loinc_code_returns_201
preconditions: A patient resource with known ID exists in fhir-lite
LOINC system is registered in the server's code system list
actions: POST /Observation with resourceType, status, subject reference, and LOINC-coded code element
Assert HTTP 201 response
Assert response body contains an id field
Assert response code.coding[0].system equals LOINC system URL
trace: R-HLT-080: clinician shall create Observation tied to patient with LOINC-coded code element

tests.name: test_create_observation_without_code_returns_422
preconditions: A patient resource with known ID exists in fhir-lite
actions: POST /Observation with resourceType and subject but WITHOUT code element
Assert HTTP 422 response
Assert response body contains an OperationOutcome or error detail mentioning code
expected: Error detail references missing or invalid code element
trace: R-HLT-080: Observations without code shall be rejected
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_create_observation_without_code_returns_422(
    clinician_auth_headers,  # <fixture>
    existing_patient_id,     # <fixture>
    fhir_base_url,           # <fixture>
):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Observation",
        "status": "final",
        "subject": {"reference": f"Patient/{existing_patient_id}"}
        # code element intentionally omitted
    }
    r = requests.post(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/Observation",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_headers,
    )
    assert r.status_code == 422, (
        f"Expected 422 but got {r.status_code}: {r.text}"
    )
    body = r.json()
    # Server must return OperationOutcome or structured error
    issue_text = str(body).lower()
    assert "code" in issue_text or "validation" in issue_text, (
        f"Error body does not reference code field: {body}"
    )

tests.name: test_create_observation_with_empty_coding_array_returns_422
preconditions: A patient resource with known ID exists in fhir-lite
code element is present but coding array contains zero items (boundary: length=0)
actions: POST /Observation with code present but coding set to empty array []
Assert HTTP 422 response (boundary: code exists but has no LOINC entry)
Assert response error references coding or code validation
expected: Server rejects the resource because coding array is empty (no LOINC code satisfies requirement)
Error detail references code or coding
trace: R-HLT-080: LOINC-coded code element required — empty coding array is the zero-item boundary
R-HLT-080: Observations without code shall be rejected

tests.name: test_audit_event_emitted_for_successful_observation_creation
preconditions: A patient resource with known ID exists in fhir-lite
actions: POST /Observation with valid LOINC-coded payload
Capture the returned Observation id
GET /AuditEvent?entity=Observation/<id> or equivalent search
Assert at least one AuditEvent references the created Observation
expected: GET /AuditEvent search returns at least one matching event
AuditEvent references the correct Observation id
trace: R-HLT-080: AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation

tests.name: test_audit_event_emitted_for_rejected_observation
preconditions: A patient resource with known ID exists in fhir-lite
actions: POST /Observation without code element to trigger rejection
Record approximate timestamp before and after the call
GET /AuditEvent filtered by agent (clinician) and recent timeframe
Assert a failure AuditEvent was recorded for the rejected create attempt
expected: At least one AuditEvent with outcome indicating failure/error exists
AuditEvent action corresponds to create attempt
trace: R-HLT-080: AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation including failures
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
from datetime import datetime, timezone

def test_audit_event_emitted_for_rejected_observation(
    clinician_auth_headers,  # <fixture>
    existing_patient_id,     # <fixture>
    fhir_base_url,           # <fixture>
    clinician_user_id,       # <fixture>
):
    before = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Observation",
        "status": "final",
        "subject": {"reference": f"Patient/{existing_patient_id}"}
    }
    r = requests.post(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/Observation",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_headers,
    )
    assert r.status_code == 422, r.text
    audit_r = requests.get(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/AuditEvent",
        params={
            "agent": clinician_user_id,
            "date": f"ge{before}",
            "action": "C",
            "outcome": "8",  # 8 = serious failure in AuditEvent outcome codes
        },
        headers=clinician_auth_headers,
    )
    assert audit_r.status_code == 200, audit_r.text
    bundle = audit_r.json()
    entries = bundle.get("entry", [])
    assert len(entries) >= 1, (
        "Expected at least one failure AuditEvent for rejected Observation create"
    )

tests.name: test_observation_state_transition_draft_to_final
preconditions: A patient resource with known ID exists in fhir-lite
fhir-lite supports Observation status transitions from preliminary to final
actions: POST /Observation with status=preliminary and valid LOINC code
Assert HTTP 201 and status=preliminary in response
PUT /Observation/<id> updating status to final
Assert HTTP 200 and status=final in response
Verify AuditEvent exists for the update operation
expected: AuditEvent recorded for the update transition
trace: R-HLT-080: AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation
